shape model output for Uranus's 1-bar isobaric surface. models are described in Mankovich et al. 2026, PSJ.
this model assumes the symmetric wind profile from Sromovsky et al. (2015, Icarus 258) Equation (2).
these solutions vary the polar radius to fit the pair of Voyager 2 occultations reported in Lindal et al. 1987 to a Gaussian standard deviation of 50 m.
the wind fit spectral coefficients are also allowed to vary within their Gaussian uncertainties following S15 Table 5 (Fit 1).
this model has l=0,2,..., 18 wind spectral cofficients:  -1.237205 -3.591704 -0.080216  0.824356  0.335959 -0.229900 -0.236294 -0.028814  0.082752  0.075862
mu=sin(phi), phi [radians], and lat_deg [degrees] refer to planetocentric latitude; lat_pg_deg gives planetographic latitude in degrees.
